revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:複合遺跡]]
[[File:Kyoto-hakkutu-huukei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Excavation in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} showing multiple layers of cultural deposits from different periods.]] '''Complex archaeological sites''' (複合遺跡, '''Fukugō Iseki''') are archaeological terms that describe sites where multiple types of {{ill|Feature (archaeology)|en|Feature (archaeology)|qid=Q814254|lt=Archaeological Feature|1=Feature (archaeology)}} from different {{ill|historical periods|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historical periods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Historical period|qid=Q11514315}} exist in stratified layers. These layers differ in {{ill|stratigraphy|ja|層位学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stratigraphy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 層序学|1=Stratigraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134783}} and are stacked on top of each other. Such sites contrast with "simple archaeological sites," which consist of features from a single period or type<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A4%87%E5%90%88%E9%81%BA%E8%B7%A1-617193 "Complex archaeological sites," Kotobank]</ref>. ==Overview== Land is typically composed of layers of {{ill|Soil|en|Soil|qid=Q36133|lt=soil|1=Soil}} and {{ill|gravel|ja|砂礫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gravel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砕屑物|1=Grus (geology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q473412}} accumulated over time. These {{ill|Stratum|en|Stratum|qid=Q12758989|lt=strata|1=Stratum}} generally follow the {{ill|Law of superposition|en|Law of superposition|qid=Q842427|lt=principle of superposition|1=Law of superposition}}, with older layers beneath newer ones. If a single plot of land has been repeatedly used by humans across multiple eras—for example, as a {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|qid=Q486972|lt=settlement|1=Human settlement}}, {{ill|burial site|ja|墳墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 墓|1=Tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q381885}}, {{ill|field|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/field|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=Paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q842623}} for cultivation, {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|qid=Q1724341|lt=kiln|1=Kiln}} for {{ill|Pottery|en|Pottery|qid=Q11642|lt=pottery|1=Pottery}}, or {{ill|fortification|ja|築城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fortification|1=fortification|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603493}}—various archaeological features ({{ill|pit dwellings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit dwellings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|qq=Pit-house|2=ja|3=竪穴式住居|1=Pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q940435}}, {{ill|Trench|en|Trench|qid=Q1852785|lt=trenches|1=Trench}}, {{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|qid=Q1421012|lt=pits|1=Prehistoric storage pits}}, etc.) may overlap. This interaction, where older features are disturbed by newer ones ({{ill|cutting relationships|ja|切り合い関係|1=Intercutting relationships|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cutting relationships|13=User:Immanuelle/Intercutting relationships|qq=}}), results in stratified {{ill|archaeological contexts|ja|遺構面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological contexts|1=archaeological contexts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114345798}} that evolve over time. For instance, the {{ill|Iba Site|ja|伊場遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iba Site|1=Iba Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11379269}} in {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, is known as a government center site ({{ill|Gunga (Japan)|en|Gunga (Japan)|qid=Q11643230|lt=Gunga|1=Gunga (Japan)}}) from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Below this, {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} settlements and {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} {{ill|Moated settlements|en|Moated settlements|qid=Q60995739|lt=moated settlements|1=Moated settlements}} were found<ref>Suzuki 2018, pp. 23–29</ref>. The area also contains sites for {{ill|stations|ja|駅家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stations|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668204}} and {{ill|ports|ja|湊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ports|1=ports|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563123}}, forming the "Iba Site Cluster"<ref name=suzuki2>Suzuki 2015</ref>. The concept of a {{ill|site cluster|ja|遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/site cluster|1=group of archaeological sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q112132467}} refers to a dense collection of individual sites treated collectively. Examples include {{ill|kofun clusters|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun clusters|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|rock-cut tomb clusters|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tomb clusters|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}}, and {{ill|kiln clusters|ja|窯跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiln clusters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯跡|1=kiln ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597731}}. While site clusters and complex archaeological sites are distinct, the terms are occasionally used interchangeably{{Sfn|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|2020|p=8|qq=}}. ==Examples== ===Sumpu Castle=== In {{ill|Shizuoka (city)|en|Shizuoka (city)|qid=Q174691|lt=Shizuoka City|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, the {{ill|Castle|en|Castle|qid=Q23413|lt=castle|1=Castle}} of the {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|qid=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa clan|1=Imagawa clan}} and {{ill|Tokugawa clan|en|Tokugawa clan|qid=Q1412769|lt=Tokugawa clan|1=Tokugawa clan}}, {{ill|Sunpu Castle|en|Sunpu Castle|qid=Q1038629|lt=Sumpu Castle|1=Sunpu Castle}}, is classified as a "castle ruin." However, beneath the castle grounds, excavations have uncovered a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, including more than 20 pit dwellings, as well as features and artifacts from the 8th to 12th centuries. These layers form the "Inner Sumpu Castle Site"<ref>Shizuoka Prefecture Archaeological Research Center, 2009, p. 14</ref><ref>Shizuoka City Board of Education, 2011</ref>. Therefore, while the castle itself may appear as a simple archaeological site, it is more accurately described as a complex site due to these stratified layers. ===Environmental Factors=== The longevity of human activity at such sites is often attributed to favorable geographical features, such as {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|qid=Q465088|lt=alluvial fans|1=Alluvial fan}}, {{ill|natural levees|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levees|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11268410}}, or stable {{ill|Plateau|en|Plateau|qid=Q75520|lt=plateaus|1=Plateau}}. The Iba Site is located on a {{ill|Beach ridge|en|Beach ridge|qid=Q2532557|lt=beach ridge|1=Beach ridge}} along the {{ill|Enshū Sea|en|Enshū Sea|qid=Q11642093|lt=Enshu-nada|1=Enshū Sea}} coastline, offering stability and proximity to key transport routes<ref>Suzuki 2018, pp. 7–11</ref>. Similarly, {{ill|Sunpu Castle|en|Sunpu Castle|qid=Q1038629|lt=Sumpu Castle|1=Sunpu Castle}} is situated on the {{ill|Shizuoka Plain|ja|静岡平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Plain|1=Shizuoka Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663028}}, a stable region shaped by the {{ill|Abe River|en|Abe River|qid=Q318136|lt=Abe River|1=Abe River}}'s alluvial fan<ref>Shizuoka Prefecture Archaeological Research Center, 2009, pp. 9–10</ref>. ==Challenges in Research== {{ill|Tadashi Saitō|ja|斎藤忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadashi Saitō|1=Tadashi Saitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500562}} notes that "the increasing number of such sites presents significant challenges for investigation"<ref>Saitō 2004, p. 380</ref>. Most Japanese archaeological sites span multiple periods and include diverse feature types<ref>[https://www.gis.pref.shizuoka.jp/?z=9&ll=34.9791%2C138.3831&t=roadmap&mp=11001&op=70&vlf=000affffff00000040 Shizuoka Prefecture Geographic Information System]</ref>. Truly "simple archaeological sites" are rare; the majority can be classified as complex sites. ==Complex Sites Overseas== ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ==Sources== * {{ill|Tadashi Saitō|ja|斎藤忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadashi Saitō|1=Tadashi Saitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500562}}, "Complex Sites," in ''Japanese Archaeological Terminology Dictionary'' (Revised Edition), 2004, [[Gakuseisha]], p. 380. [https://ci.nii.ac.jp/ncid/BA68729429 NCID BA68729429] * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Archaeological Research Center|ja|静岡県埋蔵文化財調査研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture Archaeological Research Center|1=Shizuoka Prefecture Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663074}} (2009), ''Inner Sumpu Castle Site,'' Report 203. [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/21/21314/15988_1_%E9%A7%BF%E5%BA%9C%E5%9F%8E%E5%86%85%E9%81%BA%E8%B7%A1.pdf PDF]. * {{ill|Shizuoka City Board of Education|ja|静岡市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka City Board of Education|1=Shizuoka City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662965}} (2011), ''Excavation Report on Shizuoka City Site Clusters.'' * {{ill|Toshi Suzuki|ja|鈴木敏則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshi Suzuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "The Iba Site Cluster and Ancient Transportation—Government Stations within Complex Sites," in ''Proceedings of the 18th Ancient Transportation Research Society Conference,'' 2015. * {{Cite book|author={{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|qid=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|1=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}}|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/statics/10IsekiJohoKozo.pdf|chapter=Structure of Site Information|title=Archaeological Site Database (Simplified Version)|publisher=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|year=2020|date=2020-06-12|ref=harv|qq=}} * {{ill|Toshi Suzuki|ja|鈴木敏則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshi Suzuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Pioneering Wooden Documents in Ancient Provinces—Iba Site,'' 2018, Shinseisha. ==See Also== * {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|qid=Q959782|lt=Excavation|1=Archaeological excavation}} * {{ill|Site Clusters|ja|遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Site Clusters|1=group of archaeological sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q112132467}} * {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} * {{ill|Complex Sites Overseas|ja|海外の複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Complex Sites Overseas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ==External Links== * [https://www.gis.pref.shizuoka.jp/?z=9&ll=34.9791%2C138.3831&t=roadmap&mp=11001&op=70&vlf=000affffff00000040 Shizuoka Prefecture GIS (Archaeological Sites)]
{{translated page|ja|複合遺跡|version=101491973|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:複合遺跡]]
<!--Complex archaeological sites--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q97186758}} [[Category:Archaeological sites]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Composite archaeological sites]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Composite archaeological sites]] [[Category:遺跡]]
